title: Oblongifolin C reverses GEM resistance via suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer cells
domain: biology / oncology (cancer cell biology); document type = RETRACTION NOTICE

claims.text: This PDF is a one-page RETRACTION notice (published in Experimental and Therapeutic Medicine 32:186, 2026), NOT the original research article it concerns; it contains no figures, tables, equations, statistics, or experimental data of the retracted study.
claims.quote: RETRACTION ... DOI: 10.3892/etm.2026.13181 ... Oblongifolin C reverses GEM resistance via suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer cells
claims.predicate: Inspect the document: confirm it is a retraction notice (header 'RETRACTION'), one page only, and contains no primary data figures/tables. pdfinfo reports Pages: 1; pdfimages reports only a publisher logo (88x31) and the journal masthead JPEG (233x78) — no data figures.

verification.verifier_needed: document-type / page-count check (pdfinfo Pages count + visual confirmation that no data figures/tables are present)
verification.rationale: pdfinfo returns Pages: 1; rendered page shows only the retraction text plus publisher logo and journal masthead; pdfimages lists only logo (88x31) and masthead JPEG (233x78). Header literally reads 'RETRACTION'. The substantive scientific claims of the retracted study are therefore absent from this file and cannot be checked against evidence — they are recorded as inconclusive/not_checkable below rather than fabricated.

claims.text: The retracted article was originally published as Exp Ther Med 20: 1431-1440, 2020 with DOI 10.3892/etm.2020.8856.
claims.predicate: Cross-check the cited volume/page/year/DOI of the original article against the journal's published record / CrossRef DOI resolver (10.3892/etm.2020.8856) and against the retraction's own restatement of the title and authors.

verification.verifier_needed: bibliographic / DOI-resolution check (CrossRef lookup of 10.3892/etm.2020.8856; confirm volume 20, pp.1431-1440, 2020, matching title/authors)
verification.rationale: Self-consistent within the notice (DOI prefix 10.3892/etm matches the publisher Spandidos; volume 20 in 2020 is plausible). Settled only by an external DOI resolver / journal-record lookup, which is not available offline. Internal arithmetic on the page range is consistent (1440-1431+1 = 10 pages, a normal article length).

claims.text: The page span of the retracted article (pp. 1431-1440) corresponds to a 10-page article.
claims.predicate: Arithmetic: end_page - start_page + 1 = 1440 - 1431 + 1 = 10. Verify the span is positive and plausible for a research article.

verification.rationale: 1440 - 1431 + 1 = 10; span is positive and consistent with a normal-length article. Pure T0 recompute, fully settled from the page text. (Does not verify the article exists — only that the stated range is internally coherent.)

claims.text: The retraction notice is dated/volumed 2026 (Exp Ther Med 32:186, 2026), i.e. retracted six years after the original 2020 publication.
claims.quote: EXPERIMENTAL AND THERAPEUTIC MEDICINE 32: 186, 2026 ... Copyright © 2026 Huang et al.
claims.predicate: Cross-consistency: the masthead year (2026), the copyright year (2026), the retraction DOI year-stamp (etm.2026.13181), and the original-article year (2020) should be mutually consistent; compute the retraction lag = 2026 - 2020 = 6 years.

evidence.quote: EXPERIMENTAL AND THERAPEUTIC MEDICINE 32: 186, 2026 ... DOI: 10.3892/etm.2026.13181 ... Copyright © 2026 Huang et al.

verification.verifier_needed: prose-vs-metadata cross-consistency (year fields agree; lag arithmetic)
verification.rationale: Masthead '32: 186, 2026', copyright '© 2026', and retraction DOI '10.3892/etm.2026.13181' all encode 2026 consistently; original article year is 2020; 2026 - 2020 = 6-year lag. All values transcribed directly and internally consistent.

claims.text: The copyright attributes the work to 'Huang et al.', consistent with the first author of the retracted article being Zhilong Huang.
claims.quote: Copyright © 2026 Huang et al. ... Zhilong Huang, Tingting Wang, Wenjun Xia, Qing Li, Xinlei Chen, Xiaoli Liu, Peng Wei, Wenping Xu and Meirong Lv
claims.predicate: Cross-consistency: the copyright surname ('Huang et al.') must match the first listed author surname (Huang). Verify name agreement between copyright line and author block.

evidence.location: Page 1, copyright line and author list

verification.verifier_needed: prose internal cross-consistency (copyright surname vs author list)
verification.rationale: Copyright 'Huang et al.' matches first author 'Zhilong Huang'. Author block lists 9 named authors. Fully settled from the page; trivial T2 string-consistency check.

claims.text: The retraction DOI prefix (10.3892) and the original-article DOI prefix (10.3892) both belong to the same publisher (Spandidos Publications), consistent with the masthead.
claims.predicate: Cross-consistency: both DOIs share the registrant prefix 10.3892 and the 'etm' journal token; masthead names Spandidos Publications and the journal 'Experimental and Therapeutic Medicine' (etm). Confirm prefix/token agreement.

evidence.location: Page 1, both DOI lines + publisher logo text

verification.verifier_needed: DOI-prefix / journal-token internal consistency (and optional external registrant lookup of 10.3892 -> Spandidos)
verification.rationale: Both DOIs carry prefix 10.3892 and the 'etm' token; masthead reads 'EXPERIMENTAL AND THERAPEUTIC MEDICINE' and logo reads 'SPANDIDOS PUBLICATIONS'. Internally fully consistent. The factual mapping 10.3892 -> Spandidos is T1 external knowledge but is corroborated on-page by the masthead/logo.

claims.text: The stated ground for retraction is image/data duplication: the immunohistochemical image in Fig. 1C (p. 1434) and certain western blot data in Figs. 3B and 4B were strikingly similar to data previously published by different authors at different research institutes.
claims.quote: the immunohistochemical image shown in Fig. 1C on p. 1434 and certain of the western blot data featured in Figs. 3B and 4B were strikingly similar to data that had already appeared previously in articles written by different authors at different research institutes.
claims.predicate: Integrity signal: the notice itself asserts image duplication across Fig. 1C, Fig. 3B, Fig. 4B versus prior publications. Settling whether the duplication is real requires forensic image comparison against the original article's figures (NOT present in this PDF) and the named prior articles (not identified here).

evidence.quote: Fig. 1C on p. 1434 ... Figs. 3B and 4B ... strikingly similar to data that had already appeared previously

verification.verifier_needed: forensic image-duplication analysis (e.g., ImageTwin/Proofig-style or manual overlay) comparing Figs 1C/3B/4B of the original article to the prior sources; requires the original figures and the source articles, neither of which is in this file
verification.candidate_verdict_note: The retraction CLAIM is reported faithfully; its truth cannot be independently verified from this file.
verification.rationale: The notice reliably reports the editor's stated grounds (this is a faithful transcription, T7 integrity signal). But independent confirmation of the duplication needs the original figures and the unnamed prior articles, which are absent here. The original paper IS in fact retracted (the notice is the primary record), so the meta-fact 'this paper is retracted for image concerns' is strongly supported; the underlying image forensics are not checkable from this PDF.

claims.text: The authors were asked to explain the concerns but the Editorial Office received no reply, and the Editor decided the paper should be retracted.
claims.quote: The authors were asked for an explanation to account for these concerns, but the Editorial Office did not receive a reply.
claims.predicate: Process/integrity signal: records that authors did not respond and the editor decided to retract. Verifiable only against the journal's editorial correspondence record (external, non-public); not checkable from this file.

evidence.quote: The authors were asked for an explanation to account for these concerns, but the Editorial Office did not receive a reply. The Editor apologizes to the readership for any inconvenience caused.

verification.rationale: This is an editorial statement about a private process (author non-response, decision to retract). It is internally coherent and standard COPE-style language, but there is no on-page or accessible evidence to confirm or refute the correspondence. Recorded as not_checkable rather than supported/suspect.

claims.text: SUBSTANTIVE SCIENTIFIC CLAIM (from the retracted article's title): Oblongifolin C reverses gemcitabine (GEM) resistance by suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer cells.
claims.quote: Oblongifolin C reverses GEM resistance via suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer cells
claims.section: Title of retracted article (restated in notice)
claims.predicate: Claim<->evidence support: would require the original article's dose-response, autophagy-flux assays (LC3-II/p62 western blots, autophagosome imaging), and cell-viability/IC50 data. NONE of that evidence is present in this PDF (it is the retraction notice only), so the title claim cannot be bound to evidence here.

evidence.location: Page 1, title (the only place the scientific claim appears)
evidence.quote: Oblongifolin C reverses GEM resistance via suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer cells

verification.verifier_needed: the original article's figures/tables (viability/IC50, LC3/p62 western blots, autophagy-flux assays) — absent from this file
verification.rationale: No data are attached to this notice, so the mechanistic claim cannot be supported or refuted from the file. Moreover, the article has been retracted for image-integrity reasons (Figs 1C/3B/4B), which undermines confidence in the underlying evidence even if it could be retrieved. Recorded as inconclusive (cannot bind to evidence) per instructions to use null/inconclusive over invented values rather than fabricating data-level claims.

claims.text: The retracted article reported data in at least Figs. 1 (incl. 1C immunohistochemistry on p.1434), 3 (incl. 3B western blot) and 4 (incl. 4B western blot) — i.e. the original study contained immunohistochemistry and western-blot experiments.
claims.quote: the immunohistochemical image shown in Fig. 1C on p. 1434 and certain of the western blot data featured in Figs. 3B and 4B
claims.predicate: Cross-consistency of figure references within the notice: confirms the original article had figures numbered at least 1, 3, 4 with sub-panels C and B, including IHC (1C) and western blots (3B, 4B). This is metadata about the original article restated in the notice; the figures themselves are not in this file to inspect.

verification.verifier_needed: internal reference-consistency check (figure numbering/panel labels and the page 1434 falling within the cited 1431-1440 range)
verification.rationale: Notice-internal consistency holds: p.1434 lies within the cited span 1431-1440, and panel labels (1C, 3B, 4B) are coherent. Confirms the original study used IHC and western blotting. The actual figure contents are not inspectable from this PDF, so this is limited to metadata-level consistency, not a check of the figures themselves.

paper_level_notes: CRITICAL: This PDF is NOT the research paper — it is the one-page RETRACTION notice for it. Title: 'Oblongifolin C reverses GEM resistance via suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer cells' (Huang Z, Wang T, Xia W, Li Q, Chen X, Liu X, Wei P, Xu W, Lv M). Original article: Exp Ther Med 20:1431-1440, 2020; DOI 10.3892/etm.2020.8856 (Spandidos Publications). Retraction record: Exp Ther Med 32:186, 2026; DOI 10.3892/etm.2026.13181, CC BY 4.0. pdfinfo reports Pages: 1; pdfimages shows only a publisher logo (88x31) and the journal masthead JPEG (233x78) — NO data figures, tables, equations, statistics, or SI. Therefore none of the retracted study's substantive scientific claims (dose-response, autophagy-flux assays, IC50/viability, western blots) can be bound to evidence from this file; those are recorded as inconclusive/not_checkable rather than fabricated, per the no-manufactured-errors instruction. What IS checkable here: (a) T0 page-span arithmetic 1440-1431+1=10 (supported); (b) T2 internal cross-consistency of year stamps/lag (2026 vs 2020 = 6 yr), copyright-surname vs author list (Huang), DOI prefixes/journal token (10.3892/etm), and figure-reference/page coherence (p.1434 in range; all supported); (c) T1 bibliographic facts about the original article (volume/pages/DOI) needing an external CrossRef/journal lookup (inconclusive offline); (d) T7 integrity signals — the document IS a retraction (strongly supported) and its stated ground is image duplication in Figs 1C/3B/4B vs prior work by other authors (faithfully reported, but the duplication itself requires forensic image comparison against the original figures and the unnamed source articles, neither present here -> inconclusive). REPRODUCIBILITY/DATA AVAILABILITY: not applicable to a retraction notice; no data/SI/code. The dominant, high-confidence finding is the integrity signal: this work was retracted in 2026 for apparent figure/image duplication, with no author response. Off-the-shelf checks that fully cover claims here: page-span arithmetic (c3), year-lag/percent-style cross-consistency (c4), and prose-vs-metadata name/DOI consistency (c5,c6) — all deterministic and settled on-page. Reviewer caveat: the discovery harness should flag that the corpus file for this entry contains the retraction notice rather than the article PDF; if the intent was to audit the original study's data, the 2020 article PDF (DOI 10.3892/etm.2020.8856) must be supplied separately.
